theme: Induce a small DFA that generalizes from samples
title: Small Automaton, Big Generalization: Inducing a DFA from Labeled Strings
strategies: keep the majority-class rule (ignore the strings entirely; reproduces the evaluator's weak a_triv anchor, ~0.1)

the literal prefix-tree acceptor (PTA): one state per distinct training prefix plus a reject sink for every unseen continuation; matches training perfectly but has as many states as distinct prefixes and collapses on held-out traces longer than the training log

RPNI-style evidence-based state merging: fold PTA states together whenever doing so stays consistent with every labeled example (propagating merges through shared transitions via union-find), recovering a small automaton close to the true device size that predicts unseen, longer traces

blue-fringe / EDSM-style merging that scores candidate merges by shared evidence count before committing, trading a slightly larger automaton for extra robustness against noisy or sparse coverage of some devices
